# Political Events — 1777

Constitutions, charters, and governance changes of 1777.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: category-index — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->
| Topic | Description |
|---|---|
| [Articles of Confederation](articles-of-confederation.md) | First formal governing compact of the thirteen United States, adopted November 15 1777 — established a league of friendship but lacked taxing power or enforcement authority. |
| [Flag Resolution — Stars and Stripes](flag-resolution.md) | Continental Congress resolution of June 14 1777 establishing the Stars and Stripes as the national flag — the first official definition of an American national emblem. |
| [Massachusetts Anti-Slavery Petition](massachusetts-slavery-petition-1777.md) | January 13 1777 appeal by Prince Hall and seven other African Americans invoking Revolutionary principles to demand abolition; the drafted bill died in committee, but the petition prefigured Massachusetts's 1783 judicial emancipation. |
| [Vermont Constitution](vermont-constitution.md) | First constitution in North America to ban adult slavery and extend the franchise to all adult men regardless of property, adopted July 8 1777 by the Windsor Convention. |
<!-- END GENERATED: category-index -->
